screener.label: What industry are you in?
screener.help: Free text. Used to personalize wording in later questions and the final report.

screener.label: How many employees does your company have?
If AI fixed one thing this year, what would create the most value?
screener.help: Free text. A single function, workflow, or outcome. This becomes the focus of your report.

scored.stem: How would you describe your organization's AI strategy?

options.text: We don't have one; AI comes up reactively
Exploring a few pilot ideas informally
Documented AI roadmap with executive sponsorship
AI is central to strategy, reviewed at board level

scored.stem: Who owns AI decisions at your company?

options.text: A designated leader or cross-functional committee
A C-level executive with a funded team

scored.stem: How is AI investment prioritized?
How does leadership measure AI success?

options.text: KPIs tied to specific projects (ROI, time saved)
Contribution to enterprise OKRs and shareholder value

scored.stem: How is your business data organized?

options.text: Systems of record exist but are siloed
Centralized warehouse or lake, with governance

scored.stem: How well do your business systems integrate?

options.text: Little or no integration; manual transfer

scored.stem: How would you rate your data quality?

options.text: Mixed; known issues but no remediation plan
Continuously validated, with data contracts and lineage

scored.stem: What is your cloud and compute readiness for AI workloads?
How AI-literate is your overall workforce?

options.text: Very limited; most staff have not used AI tools
A few early adopters using tools individually
Most teams trained on AI fundamentals
AI fluency is a baseline expectation org-wide

scored.stem: What AI training or enablement do you provide?

options.text: Continuous learning with certifications and internal comms

scored.stem: Do you have dedicated AI, data, or ML talent in-house?

options.text: A small team or clear ownership
A scaled function with specialized roles

scored.stem: How are teams encouraged to experiment with AI (particularly around {{priority_function}})?

options.text: Sandbox tools, approved vendors, allocated time
Innovation budget, internal hackathons, scaling pathways

scored.stem: Do you have written AI usage policies?

options.text: Formal policy covering acceptable use, data, vendors
Living policy with training, audits, and enforcement

scored.stem: How do you evaluate third-party AI vendors or tools?

options.text: Vendor risk program with AI-specific criteria (bias, data handling, model provenance)

scored.stem: How do you manage AI-related risks (privacy, accuracy, bias)?

options.text: Continuous monitoring, red-teaming, model review board

scored.stem: How does AI use intersect with your compliance obligations (in {{industry}})?

options.text: Mapped to specific regulations, reviewed by legal
Compliance automated and auditable; proactive regulator engagement

scored.stem: How many AI-powered workflows or features do you have in production today?
How do you decide which AI use cases to pursue?

options.text: Whatever sounds interesting at the time
Strategic portfolio aligned to business outcomes

scored.stem: How do you measure ROI of AI initiatives?

options.text: Continuous value tracking in finance systems

scored.stem: When a pilot succeeds, how do you scale it (particularly around {{priority_function}})?

options.text: Standard scaling playbook, operational handoff, continuous optimization
